2603.26458.title: Can AI Models Direct Each Other? Organizational Structure as a Probe into Training Limitations
2603.26458.paper_summary: Can an expensive AI model effectively direct a cheap one to solve software engineering tasks? We study this question by introducing ManagerWorker, a two-agent pipeline where an expensive "manager" model (text-only, no code execution) analyzes issues, dispatches exploration tasks, and reviews implementations, while a cheap "worker" model (with full repo access) executes code changes. We evaluate on 200 instances from SWE-bench Lite across five configurations that vary the manager-worker relationship, pipeline complexity, and model pairing. Our findings reveal both the promise and the limits of multi-agent direction: (1) a strong manager directing a weak worker (62%) matches a strong single agent (60%) at a fraction of the strong-model token usage, showing that expensive reasoning can substitute for expensive execution; (2) a weak manager directing a weak worker (42%) performs worse than the weak agent alone (44%), demonstrating that the directing relationship requires a genuine capability gap--structure without substance is pure overhead; (3) the manager's value lies in directing, not merely reviewing--a minimal review-only loop adds just 2pp over the baseline, while structured exploration and planning add 11pp, showing that active direction is what makes the capability gap productive; and (4) these behaviors trace to a single root cause: current models are trained as monolithic agents, and splitting them into director/worker roles fights their training distribution. The pipeline succeeds by designing around this mismatch--keeping each model close to its trained mode (text generation for the manager, tool use for the worker) and externalizing organizational structure to code. This diagnosis points to concrete training gaps: delegation, scoped execution, and mode switching are skills absent from current training data.
2603.26458.paper_summary_zh: 昂貴的 AI 模型能否有效地指導廉價模型解決軟體工程任務？我們通過引入 ManagerWorker 來研究這個問題，這是一個兩代理的管道，其中昂貴的「管理者」模型（僅限文本，無法執行代碼）分析問題、派遣探索任務並審查實現，而廉價的「工作者」模型（具有完整的代碼庫訪問權限）執行代碼更改。我們在來自 SWE-bench Lite 的 200 個實例上進行評估，涵蓋五種配置，這些配置改變了管理者與工作者之間的關係、管道的複雜性以及模型的配對。我們的發現揭示了多代理指導的潛力和局限性：（1）強大的管理者指導弱小的工作者（62%）的表現與強大的單一代理（60%）相匹配，但使用的強模型標記數量卻少得多，顯示出昂貴的推理可以替代昂貴的執行；（2）弱小的管理者指導弱小的工作者（42%）的表現不如單獨的弱代理（44%），這表明指導關係需要真正的能力差距——沒有實質內容的結構只是純粹的開銷；（3）管理者的價值在於指導，而不僅僅是審查——僅進行最小審查的循環相對基準僅增加 2 個百分點，而結構化的探索和規劃則增加 11 個百分點，顯示出主動指導才是使能力差距產生效益的關鍵；（4）這些行為源於一個根本原因：當前的模型被訓練為整體代理，將其拆分為指導者/工作者角色會與其訓練分佈相抵觸。該管道通過圍繞這種不匹配進行設計而成功——使每個模型保持接近其訓練模式（管理者的文本生成，工作者的工具使用）並將組織結構外部化為代碼。這一診斷指出了具體的訓練缺口：委派、範圍執行和模式切換是當前訓練數據中缺失的技能。
2603.26458.author: Rui Liu et.al.
